candidate.status: GitHub Enterprise active; new org created; 0 repos

goals.goal: Compound on Anthropic's Claude Code release cadence (rss.xml)
Enable Claude Code on the Web, routines, ultraplan, ultrareview
Run CI-triggered programmatic agents (Agent SDK in pipelines)
Keep the GitHub MCP toolset (already wired) as first-class

criterion.question: Does the platform support Claude Code on the Web out of the box (docs.claude.com)?
criterion.signal: claude-code-on-the-web docs reference GitHub PRs / issues directly

criterion.question: How mature is the Claude Code CI integration — production or beta?
criterion.signal: code.claude.com/docs/en/github-actions is GA with example workflows

criterion.question: Is the /code-review workflow documented and wired for this platform?
Do /ultraplan and /ultrareview expect GitHub PRs, GitLab MRs, or either?
Is the platform's MCP server first-party (github MCP) vs community (gitlab MCP)?
criterion.signal: This repo already uses mcp__github__* tools extensively

criterion.question: Does computer-use have documented recipes for the platform's UI?
Does the platform have a documented Enterprise integration (SSO, audit log, self-hosted)?
Which platform does this monorepo's tooling already target?
criterion.signal: claude-code-actions/, devops-review/, and GitHub hooks all target GitHub

criterion.question: Which platform's Claude Code integration adopts RSS changelog items faster?
What does claude.com/customers show about real-world usage?
criterion.signal: Anthropic docs power GitHub Copilot's Claude integration

criterion.question: Per-seat cost at current team size and the next 2 scale tiers?
What is the cost of migrating the 9 sub-repos if the initial choice is wrong?
criterion.signal: Monorepo with 9 sub-repos + submodules; nontrivial history

element.name: diff-summary
element.description: Summary of git diffs inspected this session (file count, +/- lines, branches touched).

element.name: hedge
element.description: What to keep dormant/fallback and conditions to revisit.

rubric.check: Each of the 12 criteria MUST be scored for BOTH platforms (24 rows total).
All score attributes MUST be integers in [1, 5].
Each rationale MUST be at least 40 characters and reference at least one concrete signal or doc URL.
Each platform's final-score MUST equal the weighted average of its criteria (within rounding).
recommendation/@choice MUST match the platform with the higher final-score, OR include an explicit override rationale in hedge.
diff-summary MUST be populated from actual git diffs in this session (not empty, not "N/A").
A hedge SHOULD name a concrete condition under which the unchosen platform would win.
next-actions SHOULD list at least 3 concrete, verifiable actions.

evaluator.reasoning: Opus 4.7 is used because the validation requires (a) XML structural checks, (b) numeric consistency (weighted average), (c) semantic judgement on whether rationales reference real signals. Only the last needs deep reasoning; (a) and (b) are deterministic and run as scripts first.
